name: White Ivory Cream Cake
recipeIngredient: ½ cup Coarsely chopped macadamia nuts
White macadamia nuts & Strawberries or raspberries
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter and flour two 9-inch cake pans.
2. Make the Cake: In a double boiler, melt white chocolate over hot, not simmering, water. Set aside to cool slightly.
3. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, cream butter and sugar. Beat in eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add melted chocolate and vanilla on low speed. Alternately beat in flour mixture and milk on low speed until just smooth (about 20 seconds).
4. Pour batter into prepared pans and bake for 25 - 30 minutes or until the top is golden and a cake tester inserted in center comes out clean. Set cakes on wire rack to cool for 20 minutes, then invert onto racks to cool completely.
5. Prepare Filling and Frosting: Place white chocolate in a medium bowl. In a small heavy saucepan, bring 1 ½ cups cream and butter to simmer. Pour over chocolate. Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes, then stir until smooth. Refrigerate mixture until firm enough to spread (about 1 hour).
6. Assemble: Spread bottom layer with chilled white chocolate mixture. Arrange chopped macadamia nuts over filling, and top with second cake layer.
7. In a medium bowl, beat remaining cream until firm peaks form. Fold into remaining white chocolate mixture. Spread over top and sides of cake. Arrange whole macadamia nuts around rim and base. Chill until ready to serve. Garnish each piece with fanned strawberries or raspberries.
